subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Climate Change and Extreme Weather
description: One-minute skill: Climate Change and Extreme Weather.

hook.text: Heat waves are now 5 times more likely than in 1900. Extreme weather isn't just bad luck anymore.

buildup.text: A warmer atmosphere holds 7% more moisture per degree Celsius, fueling heavier rainfall events.

discovery.text: Climate attribution science can now calculate how much climate change increased a specific event.

twist.text: Infrastructure built for 20th-century weather is failing against 21st-century extremes.

climax.text: Adaptation—upgrading infrastructure, changing building codes—is now as critical as reducing emissions.

punchline.text: The weather has changed. Our buildings, codes, and plans haven't caught up yet.

quiz.question: How much more moisture can a warmer atmosphere hold per degree Celsius?
